channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: There’s another Tesla Cybertruck recall, this time affecting over 27,000 units
item.description: Tesla’s Cybertuck has had a rough time since the vehicles were released to the public. And now things are even worse: Tesla announced on Thursday is was recalling up to 27,185 Cybertrucks because of a new default found with the vehicle.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: Elon Musk’s friendship with Diddy examined after he posted about boy’s alleged abuse
item.description: Sean 'Diddy' Combs' mentee Aubrey O'Day and others called out Musk for trying to distance himself from allegations that the music mogul sexually abused multiple victims, including a 9-year-old boy.
item.dc:creator: Martha Ross, The Mercury News

item.title: Texas sues TikTok over handling of minors' data
item.description: Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton (R) sued the social platform TikTok for allegedly operating in a way that "puts the online safety and privacy of Texas children at risk" and violates a bill that aims to protect children from harmful practices related to the use of digital services, according to a p
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: California’s new AI deepfakes law gets blocked by a federal judge. Here’s why
item.description: A new California law allowing any person to sue for damages over election deepfakes has been put on pause after a federal judge granted a preliminary injunction Wednesday blocking it. U.S. District Judge John A.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: OSINT unveiled: The technology behind the intelligence
item.description: How is the Army leveraging OSINT to strengthen its intelligence capabilities? The post OSINT unveiled: The technology behind the intelligence first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: Starlink internet service being sent to places hit by Helene
item.description: FEMA said 60 Starlink units have been sent to multiple places devastated by the hurricane, with more to come.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: The EU wants to know more about social media algorithms
item.description: Via the Digital Services Regulation (DSA), the European Commission has requested information from Youtube, Snapchat and Tiktok about which parameters their algorithms use to recommend social media content to users.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How ‘Serial’ changed true crime and podcasting forever
item.description: In 2014, the story of Adnan Syed and Hae Min Lee gripped the world. But the crime in question happened 15 years earlier. Lee, a Baltimore-area high school student, was strangled to death in 1999.
item.dc:creator: Scott Nover, Fast Company

item.title: Google's search engine's latest AI injection will answer voiced questions about video and photos
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google is injecting its search engine with more artificial intelligence that will enable people to voice questions about images and occasionally organize an entire page of results, despite the technology's past misadventures with misleading information.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Netflix is getting a new sci-fi slasher just in time for Halloween but the trailer for Time Cut is a lot like one of Prime Video’s best horror movies
item.description: Netflix has released the new trailer for a sci-fi slasher movie called Time Cut, and it's a lot like one of Prime Video's best horror movies Totally Killer.
item.dc:creator: grace.morris@futurenet.com (Grace Morris), TechRadar

item.title: The US and Microsoft disrupt a Russian hacking group targeting American officials and nonprofits
item.description: U.S. authorities and Microsoft say hackers linked to Russian intelligence targeted dozens of former military and intelligence officials, journalists and civil society groups with a sophisticated campaign designed to steal information.
item.dc:creator: David Klepper, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Let your iPhone read aloud what you can't see
item.description: The new Reader Mode in iOS 18’s Magnifier app is here to help iPhone users read something small or in poor lighting. All you have to do is snap a picture.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How not to waste 34 hours a month in meetings
item.description: None of the participants will ever forget this meeting, that much is certain. The venue was the US Department of Defense, starring a new Secretary of State responsible for procurement, who was to lead her first meeting with suppliers and colleagues from her own company.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: L’economia digitale di fronte alla sfida della regolamentazione
item.description: Questo è forse uno dei dibattiti più frequenti quando si parla di legislazione in materia digitale: l’onere normativo ostacola l’innovazione? E, di conseguenza, fino a che punto è necessario regolamentare per creare un quadro solido, ma che faciliti le nuove proposte? L’eterno dilemma contrappone te
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Meta and Australia have partnered to crack down on ‘celeb bait’ scam ads
item.description: Meta said it has taken down some 8,000 so-called “celeb bait” scam ads from Facebook and Instagram as part of a new effort with Australian banks to curb the practice.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Snapchat’s failure to protect kids revealed in New Mexico lawsuit
item.description: Snapchat failed to act on “rampant” reports of child grooming, sextortion and other dangers to minors on its platform, according to a newly unredacted complaint against the company filed by New Mexico’s attorney general. Attorney General Raúl Torrez filed the original complaint on Sept.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Yelp has quietly become a home services powerhouse
item.description: When the COVID-19 pandemic abruptly shut down normal life more than four years ago, few tech companies endured more wrenching change than Yelp.
item.dc:creator: Harry McCracken, Fast Company

item.title: What to expect from Apple this October and beyond
item.description: The Apple product machine continues to whirr, with a host of new products, including Macs, iPads, and iPhone SE, expected to appear this fall.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Beware of fraudsters posing as government officials trying to steal your cash
item.description: CISA warns of scammers impersonating its officials. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson urges people to be alert and verify contacts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Newsom throws AI regulation fight into uncertainty with veto
item.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom's (D) decision to veto a sweeping artificial intelligence (AI) bill has renewed the debate over the future of regulation of the technology, leaving different sectors of the tech industry largely in disagreement on the best path forward.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Google just made a major ChromeOS misstep
item.description: Did you ever watch the early 2000s TV series Scrubs? The show revolved around a young doctor named JD who always meant well but often got lost in his own head and ended up making ill-advised decisions.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Push to map Great Lakes bottom gains momentum amid promises effort will help fishing and shipping
item.description: What lies beneath the Great Lakes' waves is largely unknown, but there's a new push to learn more about thousands of shipwrecks, underwater infrastructure and the impacts of climate change on the bottom of the world's largest freshwater system.
item.dc:creator: Todd Richmond, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: It’s a wrap for the HoloLens 2 headset
item.description: Microsoft on Tuesday confirmed it is no longer producing the HoloLens 2, the company’s second-generation augmented reality headset first launched five years ago at Mobile World Congress (MWC) in Barcelona.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: WWW creator Tim Berners-Lee closes down his foundation
item.description: The World Wide Web Foundation, the organization whose mission has been to make the web safer and more accessible, has shut down, according to The Register. The foundation, which close its virtual doors Sept.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: New Jersey offshore wind farm clears big federal hurdle amid environmental concerns
item.description: SEA GIRT, N.J. (AP) — The federal government gave a key approval Tuesday to an offshore wind farm in New Jersey, even as residents in the town where its power cable would come ashore worry it could go through underground toxic waste that's still being cleaned up. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: WAYNE PARRY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Australia's online dating industry adopts code of conduct to keep users safer
item.description: Australia’s government says a code of conduct will be enforced on the online dating industry to better protect Australian users after research found that three-in-four people suffer some form of sexual violence through the platforms.
item.dc:creator: Rod Mcguirk, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The battle cry of 2025: Do cloud local!
item.description: Microclouds can have many names and offer many features. They can be hosted by a regional cloud provider or specialize in a specific use case, such as the rise of GPU cloud providers focusing on the exploding AI space.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Germany’s antitrust authority to look into complaint against Microsoft
item.description: German file hosting cloud service provider Nextcloud appeared to have won a major victory Monday, when the country’s antitrust authority announced it would be initiating what the company described as “the first steps towards an investigation into anti-competitive behavior by Microsoft, including the
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Arkansas sues YouTube over claims that the site is fueling a mental health crisis
item.description: LITTLE ROCK, Ark. (AP) — Arkansas sued YouTube and parent company Alphabet on Monday, saying the video-sharing platform is made deliberately addictive and fueling a mental health crisis among youth in the state.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DeMILLO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: What to stream: Coldplay, an unhinged Charlie Puth, 'Salem’s Lot,' Sarah Paulson and NHL 25
item.description: Coldplay's 10th studio album and the American Music Awards celebrating their 50th anniversary are some of the new television, films, music and games headed to a device near you.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Large language models hallucinating non-existent developer packages could fuel supply chain attacks
item.description: Large Language Models (LLMs) have a serious “package hallucination” problem that could lead to a wave of maliciously-coded packages in the supply chain, researchers have discovered in one of the largest and most in-depth ever studies to investigate the problem.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: FTC Employees Vote to Unionize, NTEU Representing 36 Agencies: A Major Shift in the Workplace
item.description: Federal Trade Commission (FTC) employees voted overwhelmingly to join the National Treasury Employees Union (NTEU), marking a significant change in the workplace. The unionization process has been underway since 2023, with concerns over telework access, politicization of the civil service, and other issues.
item.dc:creator: Michele Sandiford, Federal News Network

item.title: Explained: How Salesforce Agentforce’s Atlas reasoning engine works to power AI agents
item.description: Salesforce’s recently launched Agentforce suite of low-code tools is betting on a human-like reasoning engine called Atlas that could be a game-changer in building AI agents.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: San Francisco rolls out AI-powered cameras to combat crime
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson discusses San Francisco deploying three advanced mobile surveillance cameras to deter crime in high-risk areas.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Brazil judge makes new requests to allow X to be reinstated from suspension
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — Brazilian Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes on Friday added conditions for Elon Musk’s X to have its service reestablished in the country, one day after the social media platform said it had complied with all the judge’s demands, including naming a legal representative.
item.dc:creator: MAURICIO SAVARESE, Associated Press, ABC 4
